admin_surface.scope: Executes all safety-critical upgrades and parameter changes after a 10-day delay. VERIFIED via on-chain role-grant transaction: Operations Multisig holds PROPOSER+CANCELLER, Protocol Council holds PROPOSER+EXECUTOR, Community Multisig holds TIMELOCK_ADMIN.

timelock._note: VERIFIED via on-chain decoded role-grant transaction. 10-day delay (864000 seconds, also verified by getMinDelay()). PROPOSER held by both Operations and Protocol Council multisigs (either can propose). EXECUTOR held only by Protocol Council. CANCELLER held only by Operations. TIMELOCK_ADMIN held by Community Multisig (can replace the timelock entirely in extraordinary circumstances). Per OZ AccessControl design (non-enumerable), role members are not queryable directly — discovered via decoded role-grant transaction history.

admin_surface.scope: Approves EigenLayer Improvement Proposals (ELIPs) and queues them in the timelock. The principal proposer for protocol upgrades. 3-of-5 multisig with 2 Eigen Foundation representatives + 3 independent external members on rotating 6-month terms.

multisig._note: VERIFIED active mainnet multisig. Holds PROPOSER + EXECUTOR roles on primary timelock. Threshold and signer count from EigenLayer documentation: 3-of-5 (2 Eigen Foundation + 3 external). Pre-launch: query Safe.getThreshold/getOwners() to confirm threshold and identify signers.

admin_surface.scope: Originally the sole proposer; now serves as proposer + canceller. Can propose routine upgrades and maintenance through timelock. Can pause functionality. Holds canceller role on Protocol Council proposals — provides checks-and-balances.

multisig._note: VERIFIED active mainnet multisig. Holds PROPOSER + CANCELLER roles on primary timelock. Eigen Labs operated. Threshold/signer count per docs: 3-of-6. Pre-launch: query Safe.getThreshold/getOwners().

admin_surface.scope: Governance backstop. In normal circumstances, signers act as observers receiving updates on proposed actions. In extraordinary circumstances, can perform emergency actions including immediately executing time-critical upgrades or replacing the timelock entirely (e.g. on private key compromise). 9-of-13 — highest threshold of any multisig in EigenLayer.

multisig._note: VERIFIED active mainnet multisig. Holds TIMELOCK_ADMIN_ROLE on primary timelock — can replace the timelock entirely in extraordinary circumstances (private key compromise scenario). 9-of-13 per EigenLayer docs. Pre-launch: query Safe.getThreshold/getOwners() to confirm.

admin_surface.scope: Asymmetric pause control. Multiple pauser addresses (1-of-N call to pause). Single unpauser address (held by Operations Multisig 3-of-6 = effective 3-of-6 to unpause). This asymmetry is INTENTIONAL: easy to pause for safety, hard to unpause to prevent abuse.

controller._note: PauserRegistry contract. Pauser address verified in PauserRegistry constructor: 0x5050389572f2d220ad927CcbeA0D406831012390 (Pauser Multisig, 1-of-9 per docs). Unpauser is held by a higher-threshold multisig (Operations Multisig 0xBE1685... 3-of-6 per docs). This is the canonical example of asymmetric_multisig_thresholds — second confirmation after Lido DSM.

admin_surface._schema_observation: The PauserRegistry contract IS a role registry — `mapping(address => bool) public isPauser` for pausers + `address public unpauser` for the higher-threshold actor. Schema's `role_based` controller_type captures this acceptably with notes, but a v0.3 `members[]` field with thresholds per action type would be cleaner.

admin_surface.scope: Emergency-only pause authority. Per docs: 1-of-9 (or 1-of-8 in newer config). Cannot unpause, cannot upgrade, cannot do anything else. Single signer can trigger pause. Only purpose is fast emergency response.

multisig._note: Active Pauser Multisig. 1-of-9 per docs (asymmetric pause: any signer can pause; multisig consensus required to unpause). Pre-launch: confirm threshold and signers via Safe queries.

admin_surface.scope: Authority to whitelist new strategies (LST integrations, ERC20 strategies). Separate role from owner. Verified on-chain at 0x5e4C39Ad7A3E881585e383dB9827EB4811f6F647.

controller._note: Verified via StrategyManager.strategyWhitelister(). Custom contract — not a Safe, not a TimelockController. Likely a permissions registry. Address: 0x5e4C39Ad7A3E881585e383dB9827EB4811f6F647. StrategyManager.owner() = 0x369e6F597e22EaB55fFb173C6d9cD234BD699111 (Executor Multisig). Strategy whitelisting is delegated to a custom contract; ownership of the whitelister is unverified.

upgrade_paths._note: OpenZeppelin Transparent Upgradeable Proxy (TUP@4.9.0). All core contracts (DelegationManager, StrategyManager, EigenPodManager, Slasher, RewardsCoordinator, AVSDirectory) follow this pattern. Proxy admin is held by primary timelock. 10-day delay enforced. Currently no DAO binds upgrades — proposals come from Protocol Council and Operations Multisig only. EigenLayer is in transition from multisig governance toward more decentralized future, per official roadmap, but 'EigenGov' is still council-based.

emergency_controls.scope: Pauses functionality on contracts using OnlyWhenNotPaused modifier. Granular pause flags — can pause specific functions (deposits, withdrawals, slashing) without halting all operations. Asymmetric: 1-of-9 to pause, 3-of-6 multisig to unpause.
emergency_controls._note: When paused, deposits and withdrawals are blocked — temporarily traps user funds. The pause is bounded in scope (can be selective about which functions) but not bounded in time. Significant difference from Lido GateSeal which is one-shot and time-limited.

emergency_controls.scope: Bypass authority for time-critical situations (e.g. private key compromise). Can immediately execute upgrades that would normally require 10-day timelock, or replace the timelock entirely. Used as designed governance backstop, not a hidden capability.
emergency_controls._note: 9-of-13 threshold makes abuse very difficult. The capability is documented in EigenLayer governance disclosures, not hidden. Tradeoff: real backstop authority for emergencies vs. existence of any timelock-bypass path. Score implication: backstop reduces lock-out risk, but adds attack surface (compromise of 9 of 13 keys = total protocol takeover).

protected_actions: parameter changes including caps and slashing rates
timelocks._note: 10-day delay. Among the longest of any protocol mapped. OZ TimelockController. Proposer/Canceller/Executor roles separated across distinct multisigs — strong role separation.

Verified on-chain: getMinDelay() = 2073600 seconds = 24 days. SIGNIFICANTLY LONGER than the primary timelock (10 days). bEIGEN is the protocol token; longer delay reflects higher stakes for token-contract changes. This is a tiered-timelock pattern (similar to Aave's 1d/7d Lvl1/Lvl2 executors). Positive risk signal.

_oracles_note: EigenLayer does not use price oracles for its core operations. Slashing is determined by AVS-specific logic, not oracle pricing. AVSs may use oracles internally but those are out of EigenLayer protocol scope.

meta._governance_observation: EigenLayer exhibits the most sophisticated multisig hierarchy of any protocol mapped: 4 distinct multisigs with different scopes, ratios (3/5, 3/6, 1/9, 9/13), and roles in the timelock state machine (proposer, canceller, executor, bypass). This is genuinely good governance design — comparable to Lido's Dual Governance in ambition but multisig-based instead of token-bound DAO. The trade-off: governance binding is at the multisig layer, not the EIGEN token layer (yet — EigenGov is a stated future direction).
meta._v0_3_candidates_confirmed: EigenLayer confirms THREE v0.3 schema candidates: (1) `asymmetric_multisig_thresholds` — PauserRegistry's 1-of-9 pause vs higher-threshold unpause is the canonical example. Second confirmation after Lido DSM. LOCK-READY. (2) `role_based.members[]` structure — PauserRegistry has explicit isPauser mapping + unpauser address; pattern matches Aave ACL Manager. Third confirmation. LOCK-READY. (3) `governance_binding` enum — EigenLayer's pattern is `multisig_only` (no DAO yet, EigenGov in development) — distinct from GMX (advisory DAO + multisig) and Jupiter (hybrid). Adds a fourth value to the enum.
meta._governance_binding_for_dashboard: multisig_only — Current EigenLayer governance is purely multisig-based. EIGEN token holders do not currently bind any on-chain action. EigenGov roadmap exists but is not yet operational.

sources._note: Role assignments verified by decoding scheduleBatch/executeBatch transaction inputs from the timelock contract. OZ AccessControl is non-enumerable; role members discovered via transaction history rather than direct query.

meta._correction_note: Earlier mapping cited multisig addresses 0x1a051eF1, 0x8eD55c76, 0x841B988a, 0xC107547924 from the EigenLayer GitHub README — those addresses are NOT live on Ethereum mainnet (no bytecode). The CURRENT mainnet multisigs (verified by decoding role-grant transactions on the primary timelock) are the addresses now reflected here: Operations 0xBE1685C8, Protocol Council 0x461854d8, Community 0xFEA47018, Pauser 0x50503895, Executor 0x369e6F59. The README appears to have multiple deployment sections; the addresses I originally cited may have been for a deprecated set or a testnet.
